meta.note: 80-150 word answers in Jamie's voice, adapted from voice corpus. Review before submitting.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: I describe myself as a data-driven program manager with a master's in Organizational Psychology, and this role sits right at the intersection of what I do best—running a program end-to-end while staying close to the people inside it. I currently manage 20+ educational programs and facilitate trainings for 600+ participants, but what I find most meaningful isn't the logistics; it's being the person a learner can count on when something gets hard. A Pathmaker cohort moving from entry through to a real lab placement is exactly the kind of arc I want to own—recruiting the right people, supporting them through it, and tracking outcomes so the program keeps getting better. That's the impact I hope to bring.

answers.question: Why Northeastern / the Pathmaker program?
answers.answer: I'll be honest—what pulled me in was the mission, not just the title. Pathmaker is a state-funded program building real pathways into life-sciences careers for high school graduates, and I care deeply about that kind of work. The College of Professional Studies describes itself as a workforce-development hub at the intersection of industry and academics, and that systems mindset is exactly how I approach my own work—using data and stakeholder insight to design programs that actually stick. I'd love to help a cohort go from their first interview to their first lab role, and to do it inside an institution that treats learner success as the whole point. That alignment is rare, and it's why I'm genuinely excited about this one.

answers.question: Describe your experience managing a cohort or learning program end-to-end.
answers.answer: At InGenius Prep, I manage 20+ educational programs end-to-end—from intake and orientation through delivery and follow-up—coordinating vendors and cross-functional teams to keep each running smoothly. My approach is to diagnose before designing: I ran needs assessments with 25+ stakeholders to find where learners actually struggled, then built the guides, workflows, and touchpoints that closed those gaps, which cut onboarding time by 75%. I've also facilitated cohort trainings and orientations for 600+ participants, driving a 78% enrollment rate. Throughout, I track program data—progress, completion, feedback—and turn it into reports leaders can act on. Earlier, at NextGen Healthcare, I analyzed 2,000+ employee survey responses to shape strategy. Owning a program from the first interview through measured outcomes is the work I find most rewarding.
